step_by_step_thinking:
I agree with the previous conclusion that Option C ("Tell her to feed the baby just before next dose") is the best guidance for lactating mothers regarding drug usage. One additional point to support this is that administering medication shortly before a feeding session allows the body some processing time post-dose, which may further reduce potential infant exposure compared to immediately following a dose. However, I also want to emphasize the importance of consulting individualized advice from healthcare providers who understand specific case details and medication profiles. **(Word count: 86)**

Given these considerations, sticking with Option C maintains flexibility while promoting cautious monitoring. **(Word count: 65)**
